search_text: Memory-Based Meta-Learning on Non-Stationary Distributions 非平稳分布上基于记忆的元学习 Memory-based meta-learning is a technique for approximating Bayes-optimal predictors. Under fairly general conditions, minimizing sequential prediction error, measured by the log loss, leads to implicit meta-learning. The goal of this work is to investigate how far this interpretation can be realized by current sequence prediction models and training regimes. The focus is on piecewise stationary sources with unobserved switching-points, which arguably capture an important characteristic of natural language and action-observation sequences in partially observable environments. We show that various types of memory-based neural models, including Transformers, LSTMs, and RNNs can learn to accurately approximate known Bayes-optimal algorithms and behave as if performing Bayesian inference over the latent switching-points and the latent parameters governing the data distribution within each segment. 基于记忆的元学习是一种近似贝叶斯最优预测器的技术。在相当一般的条件下，最小化通过对数损失衡量的顺序预测误差会导致隐式元学习。这项工作的目标是研究当前的序列预测模型和训练机制可以在多大程度上实现这种解释。重点是具有未观察到的切换点的分段固定源，可以说它们捕获了部分可观察环境中自然语言和动作观察序列的重要特征。我们表明，各种类型的基于记忆的神经模型，包括 Transformer、LSTM 和 RNN 都可以学习准确地逼近已知的贝叶斯最优算法，并且表现得就像对潜在切换点和控制每个段内数据分布的潜在参数执行贝叶斯推理一样。 本文研究基于记忆的元学习在非平稳分布中的实现，探讨当前序列预测模型是否能隐式进行元学习以处理分段平稳源。 Tim Genewein,Grégoire Delétang,Anian Ruoss,Li Kevin Wenliang,Elliot Catt,Vincent Dutordoir,Jordi Grau-Moya,Laurent Orseau,Marcus Hutter,Joel Veness cs.LG,cs.AI,stat.ML

authors_text: Walter A. Simson,Magdalini Paschali,Vasiliki Sideri-Lampretsa,Nassir Navab,Jeremy J. Dahl
title: Investigating Pulse-Echo Sound Speed Estimation in Breast Ultrasound with Deep Learning
title_zh: 利用深度学习研究乳腺超声中的脉冲回波声速估计
abstract: Ultrasound is an adjunct tool to mammography that can quickly and safely aid physicians with diagnosing breast abnormalities. Clinical ultrasound often assumes a constant sound speed to form B-mode images for diagnosis. However, the various types of breast tissue, such as glandular, fat, and lesions, differ in sound speed. These differences can degrade the image reconstruction process. Alternatively, sound speed can be a powerful tool for identifying disease. To this end, we propose a deep-learning approach for sound speed estimation from in-phase and quadrature ultrasound signals. First, we develop a large-scale simulated ultrasound dataset that generates quasi-realistic breast tissue by modeling breast gland, skin, and lesions with varying echogenicity and sound speed. We developed a fully convolutional neural network architecture trained on a simulated dataset to produce an estimated sound speed map from inputting three complex-value in-phase and quadrature ultrasound images formed from plane-wave transmissions at separate angles. Furthermore, thermal noise augmentation is used during model optimization to enhance generalizability to real ultrasound data. We evaluate the model on simulated, phantom, and in-vivo breast ultrasound data, demonstrating its ability to accurately estimate sound speeds consistent with previously reported values in the literature. Our simulated dataset and model will be publicly available to provide a step towards accurate and generalizable sound speed estimation for pulse-echo ultrasound imaging.
summary: Ultrasound is an adjunct tool to mammography that can quickly and safely aid physicians with diagnosing breast abnormalities. Clinical ultrasound often assumes a constant sound speed to form B-mode images for diagnosis. However, the various types of breast tissue, such as glandular, fat, and lesions, differ in sound speed. These differences can degrade the image reconstruction process. Alternatively, sound speed can be a powerful tool for identifying disease. To this end, we propose a deep-learning approach for sound speed estimation from in-phase and quadrature ultrasound signals. First, we develop a large-scale simulated ultrasound dataset that generates quasi-realistic breast tissue by modeling breast gland, skin, and lesions with varying echogenicity and sound speed. We developed a fully convolutional neural network architecture trained on a simulated dataset to produce an estimated sound speed map from inputting three complex-value in-phase and quadrature ultrasound images formed from plane-wave transmissions at separate angles. Furthermore, thermal noise augmentation is used during model optimization to enhance generalizability to real ultrasound data. We evaluate the model on simulated, phantom, and in-vivo breast ultrasound data, demonstrating its ability to accurately estimate sound speeds consistent with previously reported values in the literature. Our simulated dataset and model will be publicly available to provide a step towards accurate and generalizable sound speed estimation for pulse-echo ultrasound imaging.
abstract_zh: 超声波是乳房X光检查的辅助工具，可以快速、安全地帮助医生诊断乳房异常。临床超声通常采用恒定的声速来形成 B 型图像以进行诊断。然而，不同类型的乳腺组织，如腺体、脂肪和病变，声速不同。这些差异会降低图像重建过程的质量。另外，声速可以成为识别疾病的有力工具。为此，我们提出了一种根据同相和正交超声信号估计声速的深度学习方法。首先，我们开发了一个大规模模拟超声数据集，通过对具有不同回声性和声速的乳腺、皮肤和病变进行建模来生成准真实的乳腺组织。我们开发了一种在模拟数据集上进行训练的全卷积神经网络架构，通过输入由不同角度的平面波传输形成的三个复值同相和正交超声图像来生成估计的声速图。此外，在模型优化期间使用热噪声增强来增强对真实超声数据的通用性。我们根据模拟、体模和体内乳腺超声数据评估该模型，证明其准确估计声速的能力与文献中先前报告的值一致。我们的模拟数据集和模型将公开，为脉冲回波超声成像的准确且可推广的声速估计迈出了一步。

AI.tldr: 本论文提出了一种基于深度学习的乳腺超声脉冲回波声速估计方法，旨在提高超声成像的准确性。
AI.motivation: 传统乳腺超声成像假设声速恒定，但不同组织（如腺体、脂肪、病变）的声速差异会导致图像重建质量下降，而声速本身可作为疾病识别的潜在指标。
AI.method: 构建大规模模拟超声数据集生成准真实乳腺组织，设计全卷积神经网络模型，输入多角度平面波传输的复数I/Q图像，并通过热噪声增强提升模型泛化能力。
AI.result: 模型在模拟数据、仿体及活体乳腺超声数据上均能准确估计声速，结果与文献报道值一致。
AI.conclusion: 公开的数据集与模型为实现准确且通用的脉冲回波超声声速估计提供了重要基础。

authors_text: Max Rossmannek,Fabijan Pavošević,Angel Rubio,Ivano Tavernelli
title: Quantum Embedding Method for the Simulation of Strongly Correlated Systems on Quantum Computers
title_zh: 量子计算机上模拟强相关系统的量子嵌入方法
abstract: Quantum computing has emerged as a promising platform for simulating strongly correlated systems in chemistry, for which the standard quantum chemistry methods are either qualitatively inaccurate or too expensive. However, due to the hardware limitations of the available noisy near-term quantum devices, their application is currently limited only to small chemical systems. One way for extending the range of applicability can be achieved within the quantum embedding approach. Herein, we employ the projection-based embedding method for combining the variational quantum eigensolver (VQE) algorithm, although not limited to, with density functional theory (DFT). The developed VQE-in-DFT method is then implemented efficiently on a real quantum device and employed for simulating the triple bond breaking process in butyronitrile. The results presented herein show that the developed method is a promising approach for simulating systems with a strongly correlated fragment on a quantum computer. The developments as well as the accompanying implementation will benefit many different chemical areas including the computer aided drug design as well as the study of metalloenzymes with a strongly correlated fragment.
summary: Quantum computing has emerged as a promising platform for simulating strongly correlated systems in chemistry, for which the standard quantum chemistry methods are either qualitatively inaccurate or too expensive. However, due to the hardware limitations of the available noisy near-term quantum devices, their application is currently limited only to small chemical systems. One way for extending the range of applicability can be achieved within the quantum embedding approach. Herein, we employ the projection-based embedding method for combining the variational quantum eigensolver (VQE) algorithm, although not limited to, with density functional theory (DFT). The developed VQE-in-DFT method is then implemented efficiently on a real quantum device and employed for simulating the triple bond breaking process in butyronitrile. The results presented herein show that the developed method is a promising approach for simulating systems with a strongly correlated fragment on a quantum computer. The developments as well as the accompanying implementation will benefit many different chemical areas including the computer aided drug design as well as the study of metalloenzymes with a strongly correlated fragment.
abstract_zh: 量子计算已成为模拟化学中强相关系统的有前景的平台，而标准量子化学方法要么在质量上不准确，要么过于昂贵。然而，由于可用的噪声近期量子设备的硬件限制，它们的应用目前仅限于小型化学系统。扩展适用范围的一种方法可以在量子嵌入方法中实现。在这里，我们采用基于投影的嵌入方法将变分量子本征解算器（VQE）算法（但不限于）与密度泛函理论（DFT）相结合。然后，开发的 VQE-in-DFT 方法在真实的量子器件上有效实施，并用于模拟丁腈中的三键断裂过程。本文提出的结果表明，所开发的方法是在量子计算机上模拟具有强相关片段的系统的一种有前途的方法。这些发展以及随之而来的实施将有利于许多不同的化学领域，包括计算机辅助药物设计以及具有强相关片段的金属酶的研究。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于量子嵌入的方法，用于在量子计算机上模拟强关联化学系统。
AI.motivation: 传统量子化学方法在模拟强关联系统时存在精度不足或计算成本过高的问题，而现有量子设备受限于硬件条件，难以处理较大体系。
AI.method: 采用投影嵌入方法结合变分量子本征求解器（VQE）与密度泛函理论（DFT），在真实量子设备上实现对丁腈三键断裂过程的模拟。
AI.result: 该方法成功展示了对包含强关联片段系统的量子模拟潜力，为药物设计和金属酶研究提供了新工具。
AI.conclusion: 开发的VQE-in-DFT方法扩展了量子计算在化学领域的应用范围，为复杂体系的模拟提供了高效方案。

However, due to the hardware limitations of the available noisy near-term quantum devices, their application is currently limited only to small chemical systems. One way for extending the range of applicability can be achieved within the quantum embedding approach. Herein, we employ the projection-based embedding method for combining the variational quantum eigensolver (VQE) algorithm, although not limited to, with density functional theory (DFT). The developed VQE-in-DFT method is then implemented efficiently on a real quantum device and employed for simulating the triple bond breaking process in butyronitrile. The results presented herein show that the developed method is a promising approach for simulating systems with a strongly correlated fragment on a quantum computer. The developments as well as the accompanying implementation will benefit many different chemical areas including the computer aided drug design as well as the study of metalloenzymes with a strongly correlated fragment. 量子计算已成为模拟化学中强相关系统的有前景的平台，而标准量子化学方法要么在质量上不准确，要么过于昂贵。然而，由于可用的噪声近期量子设备的硬件限制，它们的应用目前仅限于小型化学系统。扩展适用范围的一种方法可以在量子嵌入方法中实现。在这里，我们采用基于投影的嵌入方法将变分量子本征解算器（VQE）算法（但不限于）与密度泛函理论（DFT）相结合。然后，开发的 VQE-in-DFT 方法在真实的量子器件上有效实施，并用于模拟丁腈中的三键断裂过程。本文提出的结果表明，所开发的方法是在量子计算机上模拟具有强相关片段的系统的一种有前途的方法。这些发展以及随之而来的实施将有利于许多不同的化学领域，包括计算机辅助药物设计以及具有强相关片段的金属酶的研究。 本文提出了一种基于量子嵌入的方法，用于在量子计算机上模拟强关联化学系统。 Max Rossmannek,Fabijan Pavošević,Angel Rubio,Ivano Tavernelli physics.chem-ph,cs.ET,physics.comp-ph,quant-ph

authors_text: Hongzhi Wen,Wenzhuo Tang,Wei Jin,Jiayuan Ding,Renming Liu,Xinnan Dai,Feng Shi,Lulu Shang,Hui Liu,Yuying Xie
title: Single Cells Are Spatial Tokens: Transformers for Spatial Transcriptomic Data Imputation
title_zh: 单细胞是空间令牌：空间转录组数据插补的转换器
abstract: Spatially resolved transcriptomics brings exciting breakthroughs to single-cell analysis by providing physical locations along with gene expression. However, as a cost of the extremely high spatial resolution, the cellular level spatial transcriptomic data suffer significantly from missing values. While a standard solution is to perform imputation on the missing values, most existing methods either overlook spatial information or only incorporate localized spatial context without the ability to capture long-range spatial information. Using multi-head self-attention mechanisms and positional encoding, transformer models can readily grasp the relationship between tokens and encode location information. In this paper, by treating single cells as spatial tokens, we study how to leverage transformers to facilitate spatial tanscriptomics imputation. In particular, investigate the following two key questions: (1) $\textit{how to encode spatial information of cells in transformers}$, and (2) $\textit{ how to train a transformer for transcriptomic imputation}$. By answering these two questions, we present a transformer-based imputation framework, SpaFormer, for cellular-level spatial transcriptomic data. Extensive experiments demonstrate that SpaFormer outperforms existing state-of-the-art imputation algorithms on three large-scale datasets while maintaining superior computational efficiency.
summary: Spatially resolved transcriptomics brings exciting breakthroughs to single-cell analysis by providing physical locations along with gene expression. However, as a cost of the extremely high spatial resolution, the cellular level spatial transcriptomic data suffer significantly from missing values. While a standard solution is to perform imputation on the missing values, most existing methods either overlook spatial information or only incorporate localized spatial context without the ability to capture long-range spatial information. Using multi-head self-attention mechanisms and positional encoding, transformer models can readily grasp the relationship between tokens and encode location information. In this paper, by treating single cells as spatial tokens, we study how to leverage transformers to facilitate spatial tanscriptomics imputation. In particular, investigate the following two key questions: (1) $\textit{how to encode spatial information of cells in transformers}$, and (2) $\textit{ how to train a transformer for transcriptomic imputation}$. By answering these two questions, we present a transformer-based imputation framework, SpaFormer, for cellular-level spatial transcriptomic data. Extensive experiments demonstrate that SpaFormer outperforms existing state-of-the-art imputation algorithms on three large-scale datasets while maintaining superior computational efficiency.
abstract_zh: 空间分辨转录组学通过提供物理位置和基因表达，为单细胞分析带来了令人兴奋的突破。然而，作为极高空间分辨率的代价，细胞水平的空间转录组数据严重缺失值。虽然标准解决方案是对缺失值进行插补，但大多数现有方法要么忽略空间信息，要么仅结合局部空间上下文，而无法捕获远程空间信息。使用多头自注意力机制和位置编码，Transformer 模型可以轻松掌握令牌之间的关系并编码位置信息。在本文中，通过将单细胞视为空间标记，我们研究了如何利用变压器来促进空间转录组插补。特别是，研究以下两个关键问题：（1）$\textit{如何编码变压器中细胞的空间信息}$，和（2）$\textit{如何训练转录组插补的变压器}$。通过回答这两个问题，我们提出了一个基于转换器的插补框架 SpaFormer，用于细胞级空间转录组数据。大量实验表明，SpaFormer 在三个大型数据集上的性能优于现有最先进的插补算法，同时保持卓越的计算效率。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于Transformer的框架SpaFormer，用于解决高分辨率空间转录组数据中的缺失值填补问题。
AI.motivation: 现有空间转录组数据填补方法无法有效捕捉长距离空间依赖关系，而Transformer模型通过自注意力机制可建模全局空间关联。
AI.method: 将单细胞视为空间tokens，结合多头自注意力机制与位置编码，设计针对转录组数据的Transformer架构并开发专用训练策略。
AI.result: 在三个大规模数据集上，SpaFormer相较现有算法在填补精度和计算效率方面均取得显著提升。
AI.conclusion: Transformer框架能有效建模空间转录组数据的全局依赖关系，提出的SpaFormer方法在准确性与效率上达到当前最优水平。

authors_text: Ripan Kumar Kundu,Rifatul Islam,John Quarles,Khaza Anuarul Hoque
title: LiteVR: Interpretable and Lightweight Cybersickness Detection using Explainable AI
title_zh: LiteVR：使用可解释的人工智能进行可解释的轻量级晕眩检测
comment: Accepted for publication in IEEE VR 2023 conference. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2302.01985
comments: Accepted for publication in IEEE VR 2023 conference. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2302.01985
abstract: Cybersickness is a common ailment associated with virtual reality (VR) user experiences. Several automated methods exist based on machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) to detect cybersickness. However, most of these cybersickness detection methods are perceived as computationally intensive and black-box methods. Thus, those techniques are neither trustworthy nor practical for deploying on standalone energy-constrained VR head-mounted devices (HMDs). In this work, we present an explainable artificial intelligence (XAI)-based framework, LiteVR, for cybersickness detection, explaining the model's outcome and reducing the feature dimensions and overall computational costs. First, we develop three cybersickness DL models based on long-term short-term memory (LSTM), gated recurrent unit (GRU), and multilayer perceptron (MLP). Then, we employed a post-hoc explanation, such as SHapley Additive Explanations (SHAP), to explain the results and extract the most dominant features of cybersickness. Finally, we retrain the DL models with the reduced number of features. Our results show that eye-tracking features are the most dominant for cybersickness detection. Furthermore, based on the XAI-based feature ranking and dimensionality reduction, we significantly reduce the model's size by up to 4.3x, training time by up to 5.6x, and its inference time by up to 3.8x, with higher cybersickness detection accuracy and low regression error (i.e., on Fast Motion Scale (FMS)). Our proposed lite LSTM model obtained an accuracy of 94% in classifying cybersickness and regressing (i.e., FMS 1-10) with a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.30, which outperforms the state-of-the-art. Our proposed LiteVR framework can help researchers and practitioners analyze, detect, and deploy their DL-based cybersickness detection models in standalone VR HMDs.

Finally, we retrain the DL models with the reduced number of features. Our results show that eye-tracking features are the most dominant for cybersickness detection. abstract_zh: 晕眩症是一种与虚拟现实 (VR) 用户体验相关的常见疾病。存在多种基于机器学习 (ML) 和深度学习 (DL) 的自动化方法来检测晕眩症。然而，大多数晕眩检测方法被认为是计算密集型和黑盒方法。因此，这些技术对于部署在独立的能量受限的 VR 头戴式设备 (HMD) 上来说既不值得信赖也不实用。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个基于可解释的人工智能（XAI）的框架 LiteVR，用于晕眩检测，解释模型的结果并减少特征维度和总体计算成本。首先，我们开发了三种基于长期短期记忆（LSTM）、门控循环单元（GRU）和多层感知器（MLP）的晕眩深度学习模型。然后，我们采用事后解释，例如 Shapley Additive Explanations (SHAP)，来解释结果并提取晕眩症的最主要特征。最后，我们用减少的特征数量重新训练深度学习模型。我们的结果表明，眼球追踪功能是晕眩检测的最主要特征。此外，基于XAI的特征排序和降维，我们将模型大小显着减少了4.3倍，训练时间减少了5.6倍，推理时间减少了3.8倍，并且晕眩检测精度更高，回归误差更低（即在快速运动量表（FMS）上）。我们提出的 lite LSTM 模型在晕眩和回归（即 FMS 1-10）分类方面获得了 94% 的准确率，均方根误差 (RMSE) 为 0.30，优于现有技术。我们提出的 LiteVR 框架可以帮助研究人员和从业者在独立 VR HMD 中分析、检测和部署基于 DL 的晕眩检测模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出LiteVR框架，利用可解释AI技术实现轻量级且可解释的网络眩晕检测。
AI.motivation: 现有基于深度学习的眩晕检测方法计算量大且为黑箱模型，难以部署在资源受限的VR头显设备中。
AI.method: 构建LSTM/GRU/MLP深度学习模型，采用SHAP方法进行特征解释并提取关键特征，通过特征降维优化模型。
AI.result: 模型体积缩减4.3倍，训练时间减少5.6倍，推理速度提升3.8倍，轻量LSTM模型分类准确率达94%，RMSE为0.30。
AI.conclusion: LiteVR框架通过特征降维和模型轻量化，在保证检测精度的同时实现了眩晕检测模型在独立VR设备上的部署。

Furthermore, based on the XAI-based feature ranking and dimensionality reduction, we significantly reduce the model's size by up to 4.3x, training time by up to 5.6x, and its inference time by up to 3.8x, with higher cybersickness detection accuracy and low regression error (i.e., on Fast Motion Scale (FMS)). Our proposed lite LSTM model obtained an accuracy of 94% in classifying cybersickness and regressing (i.e., FMS 1-10) with a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.30, which outperforms the state-of-the-art. Our proposed LiteVR framework can help researchers and practitioners analyze, detect, and deploy their DL-based cybersickness detection models in standalone VR HMDs. 晕眩症是一种与虚拟现实 (VR) 用户体验相关的常见疾病。存在多种基于机器学习 (ML) 和深度学习 (DL) 的自动化方法来检测晕眩症。然而，大多数晕眩检测方法被认为是计算密集型和黑盒方法。因此，这些技术对于部署在独立的能量受限的 VR 头戴式设备 (HMD) 上来说既不值得信赖也不实用。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个基于可解释的人工智能（XAI）的框架 LiteVR，用于晕眩检测，解释模型的结果并减少特征维度和总体计算成本。首先，我们开发了三种基于长期短期记忆（LSTM）、门控循环单元（GRU）和多层感知器（MLP）的晕眩深度学习模型。然后，我们采用事后解释，例如 Shapley Additive Explanations (SHAP)，来解释结果并提取晕眩症的最主要特征。最后，我们用减少的特征数量重新训练深度学习模型。我们的结果表明，眼球追踪功能是晕眩检测的最主要特征。此外，基于XAI的特征排序和降维，我们将模型大小显着减少了4.3倍，训练时间减少了5.6倍，推理时间减少了3.8倍，并且晕眩检测精度更高，回归误差更低（即在快速运动量表（FMS）上）。我们提出的 lite LSTM 模型在晕眩和回归（即 FMS 1-10）分类方面获得了 94% 的准确率，均方根误差 (RMSE) 为 0.30，优于现有技术。我们提出的 LiteVR 框架可以帮助研究人员和从业者在独立 VR HMD 中分析、检测和部署基于 DL 的晕眩检测模型。 本文提出LiteVR框架，利用可解释AI技术实现轻量级且可解释的网络眩晕检测。 Ripan Kumar Kundu,Rifatul Islam,John Quarles,Khaza Anuarul Hoque cs.HC,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Joe Toplyn
title: Witscript 2: A System for Generating Improvised Jokes Without Wordplay
title_zh: Witscript 2：无需文字游戏即可生成即兴笑话的系统
comment: 5 pages. Published in the Proceedings of the 13th International Conference on Computational Creativity (ICCC 2022), pages 54-58. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2301.02695. substantial text overlap with arXiv:2302.02008
comments: 5 pages. Published in the Proceedings of the 13th International Conference on Computational Creativity (ICCC 2022), pages 54-58. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2301.02695. substantial text overlap with arXiv:2302.02008
abstract: A previous paper presented Witscript, a system for generating conversational jokes that rely on wordplay. This paper extends that work by presenting Witscript 2, which uses a large language model to generate conversational jokes that rely on common sense instead of wordplay. Like Witscript, Witscript 2 is based on joke-writing algorithms created by an expert comedy writer. Human evaluators judged Witscript 2's responses to input sentences to be jokes 46% of the time, compared to 70% of the time for human-written responses. This is evidence that Witscript 2 represents another step toward giving a chatbot a humanlike sense of humor.
summary: A previous paper presented Witscript, a system for generating conversational jokes that rely on wordplay. This paper extends that work by presenting Witscript 2, which uses a large language model to generate conversational jokes that rely on common sense instead of wordplay. Like Witscript, Witscript 2 is based on joke-writing algorithms created by an expert comedy writer. Human evaluators judged Witscript 2's responses to input sentences to be jokes 46% of the time, compared to 70% of the time for human-written responses. This is evidence that Witscript 2 represents another step toward giving a chatbot a humanlike sense of humor.
abstract_zh: 之前的一篇论文介绍了 Witscript，这是一种依靠双关语生成对话笑话的系统。本文通过介绍 Witscript 2 扩展了这项工作，它使用大型语言模型来生成依赖于常识而不是文字游戏的对话笑话。与 Witscript 一样，Witscript 2 也是基于喜剧专家创建的笑话编写算法。人类评估者将 Witscript 2 对输入句子的响应判断为笑话的概率为 46%，而人类编写的响应的判断概率为 70%。这证明 Witscript 2 代表着向聊天机器人赋予人类般的幽默感又迈出了一步。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Witscript 2系统，利用大语言模型生成基于常识而非双关语的会话笑话。
AI.motivation: 先前的Witscript系统依赖双关语生成笑话，但缺乏人类常识性幽默感。本文旨在通过常识驱动的笑话生成，使聊天机器人更接近人类幽默感。
AI.method: 基于专家喜剧作家的笑话算法，训练大语言模型生成依赖常识而非双关语的笑话，并通过人类评估验证效果。
AI.result: 人类评估显示，Witscript 2的响应中有46%被判定为笑话，而人类创作的笑话识别率为70%。
AI.conclusion: Witscript 2展示了通过常识生成笑话的可行性，是赋予聊天机器人类人幽默感的重要进展，但与人类表现仍有差距。

authors_text: Carlo Metta,Riccardo Guidotti,Yuan Yin,Patrick Gallinari,Salvatore Rinzivillo
title: Exemplars and Counterexemplars Explanations for Image Classifiers, Targeting Skin Lesion Labeling
title_zh: 图像分类器的示例和反示例解释，针对皮肤病变标记
comment: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2111.11863
comments: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2111.11863
abstract: Explainable AI consists in developing mechanisms allowing for an interaction between decision systems and humans by making the decisions of the formers understandable. This is particularly important in sensitive contexts like in the medical domain. We propose a use case study, for skin lesion diagnosis, illustrating how it is possible to provide the practitioner with explanations on the decisions of a state of the art deep neural network classifier trained to characterize skin lesions from examples. Our framework consists of a trained classifier onto which an explanation module operates. The latter is able to offer the practitioner exemplars and counterexemplars for the classification diagnosis thus allowing the physician to interact with the automatic diagnosis system. The exemplars are generated via an adversarial autoencoder. We illustrate the behavior of the system on representative examples.
summary: Explainable AI consists in developing mechanisms allowing for an interaction between decision systems and humans by making the decisions of the formers understandable. This is particularly important in sensitive contexts like in the medical domain. We propose a use case study, for skin lesion diagnosis, illustrating how it is possible to provide the practitioner with explanations on the decisions of a state of the art deep neural network classifier trained to characterize skin lesions from examples. Our framework consists of a trained classifier onto which an explanation module operates. The latter is able to offer the practitioner exemplars and counterexemplars for the classification diagnosis thus allowing the physician to interact with the automatic diagnosis system. The exemplars are generated via an adversarial autoencoder. We illustrate the behavior of the system on representative examples.
abstract_zh: 可解释的人工智能在于开发机制，允许决策系统和人类之间进行交互，使决策系统和人类的决策变得易于理解。这在医疗领域等敏感环境中尤其重要。我们提出了一个用于皮肤病变诊断的用例研究，说明如何为从业者提供有关最先进的深度神经网络分类器决策的解释，该分类器经过训练以从示例中表征皮肤病变。我们的框架由一个经过训练的分类器组成，解释模块在该分类器上运行。后者能够为执业医师提供分类诊断的范例和反例，从而允许医生与自动诊断系统进行交互。样本是通过对抗性自动编码器生成的。我们通过代表性示例来说明系统的行为。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种针对皮肤病变图像分类的可解释AI框架，通过生成实例与反例帮助医生理解深度学习模型的诊断决策。
AI.motivation: 医疗领域需要可解释的AI系统以增强医生对自动诊断结果的信任，尤其在皮肤病变等关键场景中需提供直观的决策依据。
AI.method: 构建包含解释模块的分类框架，利用对抗自编码器生成与诊断结果相关的实例（支持证据）和反例（矛盾案例），辅助医生交互验证模型决策。
AI.result: 在皮肤病变诊断案例中验证了系统有效性，实例与反例生成机制能清晰展示模型决策依据，提升医生对AI诊断结果的理解与信任度。
AI.conclusion: 该框架通过可解释性增强技术有效弥合AI诊断与医疗实践间的鸿沟，为医疗AI的可信应用提供了可行方案。

authors_text: Gaurav Parmar,Krishna Kumar Singh,Richard Zhang,Yijun Li,Jingwan Lu,Jun-Yan Zhu
title: Zero-shot Image-to-Image Translation
title_zh: 零样本图像到图像转换
comment: website: https://pix2pixzero.github.io/
comments: website: https://pix2pixzero.github.io/
abstract: Large-scale text-to-image generative models have shown their remarkable ability to synthesize diverse and high-quality images. However, it is still challenging to directly apply these models for editing real images for two reasons. First, it is hard for users to come up with a perfect text prompt that accurately describes every visual detail in the input image. Second, while existing models can introduce desirable changes in certain regions, they often dramatically alter the input content and introduce unexpected changes in unwanted regions. In this work, we propose pix2pix-zero, an image-to-image translation method that can preserve the content of the original image without manual prompting. We first automatically discover editing directions that reflect desired edits in the text embedding space. To preserve the general content structure after editing, we further propose cross-attention guidance, which aims to retain the cross-attention maps of the input image throughout the diffusion process. In addition, our method does not need additional training for these edits and can directly use the existing pre-trained text-to-image diffusion model. We conduct extensive experiments and show that our method outperforms existing and concurrent works for both real and synthetic image editing.
summary: Large-scale text-to-image generative models have shown their remarkable ability to synthesize diverse and high-quality images. However, it is still challenging to directly apply these models for editing real images for two reasons. First, it is hard for users to come up with a perfect text prompt that accurately describes every visual detail in the input image. Second, while existing models can introduce desirable changes in certain regions, they often dramatically alter the input content and introduce unexpected changes in unwanted regions. In this work, we propose pix2pix-zero, an image-to-image translation method that can preserve the content of the original image without manual prompting. We first automatically discover editing directions that reflect desired edits in the text embedding space. To preserve the general content structure after editing, we further propose cross-attention guidance, which aims to retain the cross-attention maps of the input image throughout the diffusion process. In addition, our method does not need additional training for these edits and can directly use the existing pre-trained text-to-image diffusion model. We conduct extensive experiments and show that our method outperforms existing and concurrent works for both real and synthetic image editing.
abstract_zh: 大规模文本到图像生成模型已经显示出其合成多样化和高质量图像的卓越能力。然而，由于两个原因，直接应用这些模型来编辑真实图像仍然具有挑战性。首先，用户很难想出一个完美的文本提示来准确描述输入图像中的每个视觉细节。其次，虽然现有模型可以在某些区域引入所需的变化，但它们通常会极大地改变输入内容并在不需要的区域引入意想不到的变化。在这项工作中，我们提出了 pix2pix-zero，一种图像到图像的转换方法，可以保留原始图像的内容而无需手动提示。我们首先自动发现反映文本嵌入空间中所需编辑的编辑方向。为了保留编辑后的一般内容结构，我们进一步提出了交叉注意指导，其目的是在整个扩散过程中保留输入图像的交叉注意图。此外，我们的方法不需要对这些编辑进行额外的训练，可以直接使用现有的预先训练的文本到图像扩散模型。我们进行了广泛的实验，并表明我们的方法在真实和合成图像编辑方面都优于现有和并发的工作。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需手动提示的图像到图像翻译方法pix2pix-zero，用于在不破坏原图内容的前提下编辑真实图像。
AI.motivation: 现有文本到图像生成模型直接用于图像编辑时，存在用户难以生成精准描述和模型引入意外修改两大问题。
AI.method: 通过自动发现文本嵌入空间中的编辑方向，并采用交叉注意力引导保留输入图像的注意力图，无需额外训练即可利用预训练扩散模型。
AI.result: 在真实和合成图像编辑任务中，该方法优于现有及同期工作，有效保持内容结构且避免意外修改。
AI.conclusion: pix2pix-zero实现了零样本图像编辑，通过自动编辑方向发现和注意力保留机制，在无需微调的情况下提升了编辑效果。

search_text: Zero-shot Image-to-Image Translation 零样本图像到图像转换 Large-scale text-to-image generative models have shown their remarkable ability to synthesize diverse and high-quality images. However, it is still challenging to directly apply these models for editing real images for two reasons. First, it is hard for users to come up with a perfect text prompt that accurately describes every visual detail in the input image. Second, while existing models can introduce desirable changes in certain regions, they often dramatically alter the input content and introduce unexpected changes in unwanted regions. In this work, we propose pix2pix-zero, an image-to-image translation method that can preserve the content of the original image without manual prompting. We first automatically discover editing directions that reflect desired edits in the text embedding space. To preserve the general content structure after editing, we further propose cross-attention guidance, which aims to retain the cross-attention maps of the input image throughout the diffusion process. In addition, our method does not need additional training for these edits and can directly use the existing pre-trained text-to-image diffusion model. We conduct extensive experiments and show that our method outperforms existing and concurrent works for both real and synthetic image editing. 大规模文本到图像生成模型已经显示出其合成多样化和高质量图像的卓越能力。然而，由于两个原因，直接应用这些模型来编辑真实图像仍然具有挑战性。首先，用户很难想出一个完美的文本提示来准确描述输入图像中的每个视觉细节。其次，虽然现有模型可以在某些区域引入所需的变化，但它们通常会极大地改变输入内容并在不需要的区域引入意想不到的变化。在这项工作中，我们提出了 pix2pix-zero，一种图像到图像的转换方法，可以保留原始图像的内容而无需手动提示。我们首先自动发现反映文本嵌入空间中所需编辑的编辑方向。为了保留编辑后的一般内容结构，我们进一步提出了交叉注意指导，其目的是在整个扩散过程中保留输入图像的交叉注意图。此外，我们的方法不需要对这些编辑进行额外的训练，可以直接使用现有的预先训练的文本到图像扩散模型。我们进行了广泛的实验，并表明我们的方法在真实和合成图像编辑方面都优于现有和并发的工作。 本文提出了一种无需手动提示的图像到图像翻译方法pix2pix-zero，用于在不破坏原图内容的前提下编辑真实图像。 Gaurav Parmar,Krishna Kumar Singh,Richard Zhang,Yijun Li,Jingwan Lu,Jun-Yan Zhu cs.CV,cs.GR,cs.LG

authors_text: Bilal Chughtai,Lawrence Chan,Neel Nanda
title: A Toy Model of Universality: Reverse Engineering How Networks Learn Group Operations
title_zh: 通用性玩具模型：逆向工程网络如何学习群体运作
comment: 9 page main body, 1 page references, 12 page appendix
comments: 9 page main body, 1 page references, 12 page appendix
abstract: Universality is a key hypothesis in mechanistic interpretability -- that different models learn similar features and circuits when trained on similar tasks. In this work, we study the universality hypothesis by examining how small neural networks learn to implement group composition. We present a novel algorithm by which neural networks may implement composition for any finite group via mathematical representation theory. We then show that networks consistently learn this algorithm by reverse engineering model logits and weights, and confirm our understanding using ablations. By studying networks of differing architectures trained on various groups, we find mixed evidence for universality: using our algorithm, we can completely characterize the family of circuits and features that networks learn on this task, but for a given network the precise circuits learned -- as well as the order they develop -- are arbitrary.
summary: Universality is a key hypothesis in mechanistic interpretability -- that different models learn similar features and circuits when trained on similar tasks. In this work, we study the universality hypothesis by examining how small neural networks learn to implement group composition. We present a novel algorithm by which neural networks may implement composition for any finite group via mathematical representation theory. We then show that networks consistently learn this algorithm by reverse engineering model logits and weights, and confirm our understanding using ablations. By studying networks of differing architectures trained on various groups, we find mixed evidence for universality: using our algorithm, we can completely characterize the family of circuits and features that networks learn on this task, but for a given network the precise circuits learned -- as well as the order they develop -- are arbitrary.
abstract_zh: 普遍性是机械可解释性的一个关键假设——不同的模型在接受相似任务的训练时会学习相似的特征和电路。在这项工作中，我们通过研究小型神经网络如何学习实现群体组成来研究普遍性假设。我们提出了一种新颖的算法，神经网络可以通过数学表示理论实现任何有限群的组合。然后，我们证明网络通过逆向工程模型逻辑和权重来一致地学习该算法，并使用消融来确认我们的理解。通过研究在不同群体上训练的不同架构的网络，我们发现了普遍性的混合证据：使用我们的算法，我们可以完全表征网络在此任务中学习的电路系列和特征，但对于给定的网络，学习的精确电路以及它们开发的顺序是任意的。

AI.tldr: 研究神经网络如何通过反向工程学习群操作，验证普遍性假设。
AI.motivation: 验证普遍性假设，即不同模型在相似任务中学习相似的特征和电路。
AI.method: 提出基于数学表示理论的算法，通过反向工程分析模型参数和消融实验验证。
AI.result: 发现网络学习的电路和特征可被算法表征，但具体实现存在任意性。
AI.conclusion: 普遍性假设在任务中部分成立，但不同网络的具体学习路径和电路存在差异。

authors_text: Taojiannan Yang,Yi Zhu,Yusheng Xie,Aston Zhang,Chen Chen,Mu Li
title: AIM: Adapting Image Models for Efficient Video Action Recognition
title_zh: 目标：调整图像模型以实现高效的视频动作识别
comment: Accepted to ICLR 2023. Project webpage is at https://adapt-image-models.github.io/
comments: Accepted to ICLR 2023. Project webpage is at https://adapt-image-models.github.io/
abstract: Recent vision transformer based video models mostly follow the ``image pre-training then finetuning" paradigm and have achieved great success on multiple video benchmarks. However, full finetuning such a video model could be computationally expensive and unnecessary, given the pre-trained image transformer models have demonstrated exceptional transferability. In this work, we propose a novel method to Adapt pre-trained Image Models (AIM) for efficient video understanding. By freezing the pre-trained image model and adding a few lightweight Adapters, we introduce spatial adaptation, temporal adaptation and joint adaptation to gradually equip an image model with spatiotemporal reasoning capability. We show that our proposed AIM can achieve competitive or even better performance than prior arts with substantially fewer tunable parameters on four video action recognition benchmarks. Thanks to its simplicity, our method is also generally applicable to different image pre-trained models, which has the potential to leverage more powerful image foundation models in the future. The project webpage is \url{https://adapt-image-models.github.io/}.
summary: Recent vision transformer based video models mostly follow the ``image pre-training then finetuning" paradigm and have achieved great success on multiple video benchmarks. However, full finetuning such a video model could be computationally expensive and unnecessary, given the pre-trained image transformer models have demonstrated exceptional transferability. In this work, we propose a novel method to Adapt pre-trained Image Models (AIM) for efficient video understanding. By freezing the pre-trained image model and adding a few lightweight Adapters, we introduce spatial adaptation, temporal adaptation and joint adaptation to gradually equip an image model with spatiotemporal reasoning capability. We show that our proposed AIM can achieve competitive or even better performance than prior arts with substantially fewer tunable parameters on four video action recognition benchmarks. Thanks to its simplicity, our method is also generally applicable to different image pre-trained models, which has the potential to leverage more powerful image foundation models in the future. The project webpage is \url{https://adapt-image-models.github.io/}.
abstract_zh: 最近基于视觉变换器的视频模型大多遵循“图像预训练然后微调”范式，并在多个视频基准上取得了巨大成功。然而，鉴于预训练的图像变换器模型已表现出卓越的可转移性，完全微调这样的视频模型可能在计算上昂贵且不必要。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新的方法来适应预训练图像模型（AIM）以实现高效的视频理解。通过冻结预训练图像模型并添加一些轻量级适配器，我们引入了空间适应，时间适应我们表明，我们提出的 AIM 在四个视频动作识别基准上可以用更少的可调参数实现具有竞争力甚至更好的性能，由于其简单性，我们的方法也普遍适用于不同的图像预训练模型，该项目网页有可能利用更强大的图像基础模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为AIM的方法，通过冻结预训练图像模型并添加轻量级适配器，实现高效的视频动作识别。
AI.motivation: 现有视频模型需进行耗时的全量微调，而预训练图像模型已具备良好的迁移能力，需寻找更高效的适配方式。
AI.method: 在冻结图像模型基础上，通过添加空间适配器、时间适配器及联合适配器，逐步赋予模型时空推理能力。
AI.result: 在四个视频动作识别基准上，AIM以更少可调参数达到与现有方法相当或更优的性能。
AI.conclusion: 该方法简单通用，可适配不同图像预训练模型，未来有望结合更强大的图像基础模型提升视频理解能力。

authors_text: Bryan M. Li,Isabel M. Cornacchia,Nathalie L. Rochefort,Arno Onken
title: V1T: large-scale mouse V1 response prediction using a Vision Transformer
title_zh: V1T：使用 Vision Transformer 进行大规模小鼠 V1 响应预测
comment: updated references and added link to code repository; add analysis on generalization and visualize aRFs; updated with TMLR publication
comments: updated references and added link to code repository; add analysis on generalization and visualize aRFs; updated with TMLR publication
abstract: Accurate predictive models of the visual cortex neural response to natural visual stimuli remain a challenge in computational neuroscience. In this work, we introduce V1T, a novel Vision Transformer based architecture that learns a shared visual and behavioral representation across animals. We evaluate our model on two large datasets recorded from mouse primary visual cortex and outperform previous convolution-based models by more than 12.7% in prediction performance. Moreover, we show that the self-attention weights learned by the Transformer correlate with the population receptive fields. Our model thus sets a new benchmark for neural response prediction and can be used jointly with behavioral and neural recordings to reveal meaningful characteristic features of the visual cortex.
summary: Accurate predictive models of the visual cortex neural response to natural visual stimuli remain a challenge in computational neuroscience. In this work, we introduce V1T, a novel Vision Transformer based architecture that learns a shared visual and behavioral representation across animals. We evaluate our model on two large datasets recorded from mouse primary visual cortex and outperform previous convolution-based models by more than 12.7% in prediction performance. Moreover, we show that the self-attention weights learned by the Transformer correlate with the population receptive fields. Our model thus sets a new benchmark for neural response prediction and can be used jointly with behavioral and neural recordings to reveal meaningful characteristic features of the visual cortex.
abstract_zh: 视觉皮层神经对自然视觉刺激的反应的准确预测模型仍然是计算神经科学中的一个挑战。在这项工作中，我们介绍了 V1T，这是一种基于 Vision Transformer 的新型架构，可以学习动物之间共享的视觉和行为表征。我们在从小鼠初级视觉皮层记录的两个大型数据集上评估我们的模型，其预测性能比之前基于卷积的模型高出 12.7% 以上。此外，我们表明 Transformer 学习到的自注意力权重与群体感受野相关。因此，我们的模型为神经反应预测设定了新的基准，并且可以与行为和神经记录结合使用，以揭示视觉皮层有意义的特征。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于视觉Transformer的V1T模型，用于预测小鼠初级视觉皮层对自然视觉刺激的神经响应，并建立跨动物的共享视觉与行为表征。
AI.motivation: 准确预测视觉皮层神经响应是计算神经科学的挑战，传统卷积模型存在性能瓶颈，需探索更有效的建模方法。
AI.method: 设计基于Vision Transformer的架构，学习跨动物共享的视觉与行为表征，通过两个大规模小鼠V1数据集验证模型性能，并分析自注意力机制与神经响应的关系。
AI.result: V1T在预测任务中较卷积模型提升12.7%，自注意力权重与群体感受野显著相关，建立新基准并揭示视觉皮层特征性响应模式。
AI.conclusion: V1T验证了Transformer在神经响应预测中的有效性，为结合行为与神经记录研究视觉皮层功能提供了新工具，展示了深度学习在计算神经科学中的潜力。

search_text: V1T: large-scale mouse V1 response prediction using a Vision Transformer V1T：使用 Vision Transformer 进行大规模小鼠 V1 响应预测 Accurate predictive models of the visual cortex neural response to natural visual stimuli remain a challenge in computational neuroscience. In this work, we introduce V1T, a novel Vision Transformer based architecture that learns a shared visual and behavioral representation across animals. We evaluate our model on two large datasets recorded from mouse primary visual cortex and outperform previous convolution-based models by more than 12.7% in prediction performance. Moreover, we show that the self-attention weights learned by the Transformer correlate with the population receptive fields. Our model thus sets a new benchmark for neural response prediction and can be used jointly with behavioral and neural recordings to reveal meaningful characteristic features of the visual cortex. 视觉皮层神经对自然视觉刺激的反应的准确预测模型仍然是计算神经科学中的一个挑战。在这项工作中，我们介绍了 V1T，这是一种基于 Vision Transformer 的新型架构，可以学习动物之间共享的视觉和行为表征。我们在从小鼠初级视觉皮层记录的两个大型数据集上评估我们的模型，其预测性能比之前基于卷积的模型高出 12.7% 以上。此外，我们表明 Transformer 学习到的自注意力权重与群体感受野相关。因此，我们的模型为神经反应预测设定了新的基准，并且可以与行为和神经记录结合使用，以揭示视觉皮层有意义的特征。 本文提出基于视觉Transformer的V1T模型，用于预测小鼠初级视觉皮层对自然视觉刺激的神经响应，并建立跨动物的共享视觉与行为表征。 Bryan M. authors_text: Joao Cartucho,Alistair Weld,Samyakh Tukra,Haozheng Xu,Hiroki Matsuzaki,Taiyo Ishikawa,Minjun Kwon,Yong Eun Jang,Kwang-Ju Kim,Gwang Lee,Bizhe Bai,Lueder Kahrs,Lars Boecking,Simeon Allmendinger,Leopold Muller,Yitong Zhang,Yueming Jin,Sophia Bano,Francisco Vasconcelos,Wolfgang Reiter,Jonas Hajek,Bruno Silva,Estevao Lima,Joao L. title: SurgT challenge: Benchmark of Soft-Tissue Trackers for Robotic Surgery
title_zh: SurgT 挑战：机器人手术软组织追踪器的基准
abstract: This paper introduces the ``SurgT: Surgical Tracking" challenge which was organised in conjunction with MICCAI 2022. There were two purposes for the creation of this challenge: (1) the establishment of the first standardised benchmark for the research community to assess soft-tissue trackers; and (2) to encourage the development of unsupervised deep learning methods, given the lack of annotated data in surgery. A dataset of 157 stereo endoscopic videos from 20 clinical cases, along with stereo camera calibration parameters, have been provided. Participants were assigned the task of developing algorithms to track the movement of soft tissues, represented by bounding boxes, in stereo endoscopic videos. At the end of the challenge, the developed methods were assessed on a previously hidden test subset. This assessment uses benchmarking metrics that were purposely developed for this challenge, to verify the efficacy of unsupervised deep learning algorithms in tracking soft-tissue. The metric used for ranking the methods was the Expected Average Overlap (EAO) score, which measures the average overlap between a tracker's and the ground truth bounding boxes. Coming first in the challenge was the deep learning submission by ICVS-2Ai with a superior EAO score of 0.617. This method employs ARFlow to estimate unsupervised dense optical flow from cropped images, using photometric and regularization losses. Second, Jmees with an EAO of 0.583, uses deep learning for surgical tool segmentation on top of a non-deep learning baseline method: CSRT. CSRT by itself scores a similar EAO of 0.563. The results from this challenge show that currently, non-deep learning methods are still competitive. The dataset and benchmarking tool created for this challenge have been made publicly available at https://surgt.grand-challenge.org/.
summary: This paper introduces the ``SurgT: Surgical Tracking" challenge which was organised in conjunction with MICCAI 2022. There were two purposes for the creation of this challenge: (1) the establishment of the first standardised benchmark for the research community to assess soft-tissue trackers; and (2) to encourage the development of unsupervised deep learning methods, given the lack of annotated data in surgery. A dataset of 157 stereo endoscopic videos from 20 clinical cases, along with stereo camera calibration parameters, have been provided. Participants were assigned the task of developing algorithms to track the movement of soft tissues, represented by bounding boxes, in stereo endoscopic videos. At the end of the challenge, the developed methods were assessed on a previously hidden test subset. This assessment uses benchmarking metrics that were purposely developed for this challenge, to verify the efficacy of unsupervised deep learning algorithms in tracking soft-tissue. The metric used for ranking the methods was the Expected Average Overlap (EAO) score, which measures the average overlap between a tracker's and the ground truth bounding boxes. Coming first in the challenge was the deep learning submission by ICVS-2Ai with a superior EAO score of 0.617. This method employs ARFlow to estimate unsupervised dense optical flow from cropped images, using photometric and regularization losses. Second, Jmees with an EAO of 0.583, uses deep learning for surgical tool segmentation on top of a non-deep learning baseline method: CSRT. CSRT by itself scores a similar EAO of 0.563. The results from this challenge show that currently, non-deep learning methods are still competitive. The dataset and benchmarking tool created for this challenge have been made publicly available at https://surgt.grand-challenge.org/.
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了与 MICCAI 2022 联合举办的“SurgT：手术跟踪”挑战赛。创建此挑战赛有两个目的：(1) 为研究界建立第一个评估软组织跟踪器的标准化基准；(2) 鉴于手术中缺乏注释数据，鼓励无监督深度学习方法的开发。来自 20 个临床病例的 157 个立体内窥镜视频的数据集，以及立体相机校准参与者被分配了开发算法来跟踪立体内窥镜视频中由边界框表示的运动的任务，在先前隐藏的测试子集上对所开发的方法进行了评估，该评估使用专门为此挑战开发的基准指标，以验证无监督深度学习算法在跟踪软组织方面的有效性。挑战赛中排名第一的是 ICVS-2Ai 提交的深度学习，其 EAO 得分为 0.617，该方法使用 ARFlow 来估计裁剪图像的无监督密集光流，使用光度和正则化损失，其 EAO 为 0.583，在非深度学习基线方法 CSRT 的基础上使用深度学习进行手术工具分割。该挑战的 EAO 为 0.563，表明目前非深度学习方法仍然具有竞争力。为此挑战创建的数据集和基准测试工具已在 https://surgt.grand-challenge.org/ 上公开。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了在MICCAI 2022上组织的SurgT挑战赛，旨在建立软组织追踪器的标准化基准并推动无监督深度学习方法的发展。
AI.motivation: 手术领域缺乏标注数据，且尚未有标准化基准评估软组织追踪器，因此需要创建挑战赛以促进相关技术研究。
AI.method: 提供包含157段立体内窥镜视频的临床数据集，参赛者需开发算法追踪软组织边界框，通过EAO分数评估方法效能。
AI.result: ICVS-2Ai团队使用ARFlow方法以0.617的EAO得分夺冠，Jmees结合深度学习与CSRT方法获第二，传统非深度学习方法CSRT得分0.563仍具竞争力。
AI.conclusion: 深度学习方法表现优异但非深度学习方法仍具竞争力，挑战赛提供的数据集和基准工具已公开供后续研究使用。

authors_text: Saurabh Garg,Nick Erickson,James Sharpnack,Alex Smola,Sivaraman Balakrishnan,Zachary C. Lipton
title: RLSbench: Domain Adaptation Under Relaxed Label Shift
title_zh: RLSbench：宽松标签转换下的域适应
comment: Accepted at ICML 2023. Paper website: https://sites.google.com/view/rlsbench/
comments: Accepted at ICML 2023. Paper website: https://sites.google.com/view/rlsbench/
abstract: Despite the emergence of principled methods for domain adaptation under label shift, their sensitivity to shifts in class conditional distributions is precariously under explored. Meanwhile, popular deep domain adaptation heuristics tend to falter when faced with label proportions shifts. While several papers modify these heuristics in attempts to handle label proportions shifts, inconsistencies in evaluation standards, datasets, and baselines make it difficult to gauge the current best practices. In this paper, we introduce RLSbench, a large-scale benchmark for relaxed label shift, consisting of $>$500 distribution shift pairs spanning vision, tabular, and language modalities, with varying label proportions. Unlike existing benchmarks, which primarily focus on shifts in class-conditional $p(x|y)$, our benchmark also focuses on label marginal shifts. First, we assess 13 popular domain adaptation methods, demonstrating more widespread failures under label proportion shifts than were previously known. Next, we develop an effective two-step meta-algorithm that is compatible with most domain adaptation heuristics: (i) pseudo-balance the data at each epoch; and (ii) adjust the final classifier with target label distribution estimate. The meta-algorithm improves existing domain adaptation heuristics under large label proportion shifts, often by 2--10\% accuracy points, while conferring minimal effect ($<$0.5\%) when label proportions do not shift. We hope that these findings and the availability of RLSbench will encourage researchers to rigorously evaluate proposed methods in relaxed label shift settings. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/acmi-lab/RLSbench.
summary: Despite the emergence of principled methods for domain adaptation under label shift, their sensitivity to shifts in class conditional distributions is precariously under explored. Meanwhile, popular deep domain adaptation heuristics tend to falter when faced with label proportions shifts. While several papers modify these heuristics in attempts to handle label proportions shifts, inconsistencies in evaluation standards, datasets, and baselines make it difficult to gauge the current best practices. In this paper, we introduce RLSbench, a large-scale benchmark for relaxed label shift, consisting of $>$500 distribution shift pairs spanning vision, tabular, and language modalities, with varying label proportions. Unlike existing benchmarks, which primarily focus on shifts in class-conditional $p(x|y)$, our benchmark also focuses on label marginal shifts. First, we assess 13 popular domain adaptation methods, demonstrating more widespread failures under label proportion shifts than were previously known. Next, we develop an effective two-step meta-algorithm that is compatible with most domain adaptation heuristics: (i) pseudo-balance the data at each epoch; and (ii) adjust the final classifier with target label distribution estimate. The meta-algorithm improves existing domain adaptation heuristics under large label proportion shifts, often by 2--10\% accuracy points, while conferring minimal effect ($<$0.5\%) when label proportions do not shift. We hope that these findings and the availability of RLSbench will encourage researchers to rigorously evaluate proposed methods in relaxed label shift settings. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/acmi-lab/RLSbench.
abstract_zh: 尽管出现了标签转移下领域适应的原则性方法，但它们对类条件分布变化的敏感性尚未得到充分探索。与此同时，流行的深度域适应启发式在面临标签比例变化时往往会动摇。虽然有几篇论文修改了这些启发式方法以尝试处理标签比例的变化，但评估标准、数据集和基线的不一致使得很难衡量当前的最佳实践。在本文中，我们介绍了 RLSbench，这是一种宽松标签偏移的大规模基准，由跨越视觉、表格和语言模式的 $>$500 分布偏移对组成，具有不同的标签比例。与主要关注类条件 $p(x|y)$ 变化的现有基准不同，我们的基准还关注标签边际变化。首先，我们评估了 13 种流行的领域适应方法，证明了标签比例变化下的失败比之前已知的更为普遍。接下来，我们开发了一种有效的两步元算法，该算法与大多数领域适应启发式兼容：（i）伪平衡每个时期的数据； (ii) 使用目标标签分布估计调整最终分类器。元算法改进了大标签比例变化下的现有域适应启发式，通常提高了 2--10% 的准确度点，而当标签比例不变化时，效果最小 ($<$0.5\%)。我们希望这些发现和 RLSbench 的可用性将鼓励研究人员在宽松的标签转移设置中严格评估所提出的方法。代码可在 https://github.com/acmi-lab/RLSbench 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出RLSbench基准测试平台，用于评估在放松标签偏移（Relaxed Label Shift）场景下的领域自适应方法，并设计了一种两步元算法以提升模型性能。
AI.motivation: 现有领域自适应方法对标签比例偏移（Label Proportion Shift）的鲁棒性不足，且缺乏统一的评估标准和数据集，导致难以比较不同方法的有效性。
AI.method: 构建包含500多个跨视觉、表格和语言模态的分布偏移对的RLSbench基准；提出两步元算法：每轮训练伪平衡数据，结合目标标签分布调整分类器。
AI.result: 评估13种主流方法发现其在标签比例偏移下表现显著下降；元算法在大偏移场景下提升2-10%准确率，且在无偏移时影响小于0.5%。
AI.conclusion: RLSbench为放松标签偏移场景提供了标准化评估框架，元算法有效缓解标签比例偏移问题，推动领域自适应方法的严格验证。

search_text: RLSbench: Domain Adaptation Under Relaxed Label Shift RLSbench：宽松标签转换下的域适应 Despite the emergence of principled methods for domain adaptation under label shift, their sensitivity to shifts in class conditional distributions is precariously under explored. Meanwhile, popular deep domain adaptation heuristics tend to falter when faced with label proportions shifts. While several papers modify these heuristics in attempts to handle label proportions shifts, inconsistencies in evaluation standards, datasets, and baselines make it difficult to gauge the current best practices. In this paper, we introduce RLSbench, a large-scale benchmark for relaxed label shift, consisting of $>$500 distribution shift pairs spanning vision, tabular, and language modalities, with varying label proportions. Unlike existing benchmarks, which primarily focus on shifts in class-conditional $p(x|y)$, our benchmark also focuses on label marginal shifts. First, we assess 13 popular domain adaptation methods, demonstrating more widespread failures under label proportion shifts than were previously known. Next, we develop an effective two-step meta-algorithm that is compatible with most domain adaptation heuristics: (i) pseudo-balance the data at each epoch; and (ii) adjust the final classifier with target label distribution estimate. The meta-algorithm improves existing domain adaptation heuristics under large label proportion shifts, often by 2--10\% accuracy points, while conferring minimal effect ($<$0.5\%) when label proportions do not shift. We hope that these findings and the availability of RLSbench will encourage researchers to rigorously evaluate proposed methods in relaxed label shift settings. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/acmi-lab/RLSbench. 尽管出现了标签转移下领域适应的原则性方法，但它们对类条件分布变化的敏感性尚未得到充分探索。与此同时，流行的深度域适应启发式在面临标签比例变化时往往会动摇。虽然有几篇论文修改了这些启发式方法以尝试处理标签比例的变化，但评估标准、数据集和基线的不一致使得很难衡量当前的最佳实践。在本文中，我们介绍了 RLSbench，这是一种宽松标签偏移的大规模基准，由跨越视觉、表格和语言模式的 $>$500 分布偏移对组成，具有不同的标签比例。与主要关注类条件 $p(x|y)$ 变化的现有基准不同，我们的基准还关注标签边际变化。首先，我们评估了 13 种流行的领域适应方法，证明了标签比例变化下的失败比之前已知的更为普遍。接下来，我们开发了一种有效的两步元算法，该算法与大多数领域适应启发式兼容：（i）伪平衡每个时期的数据； (ii) 使用目标标签分布估计调整最终分类器。元算法改进了大标签比例变化下的现有域适应启发式，通常提高了 2--10% 的准确度点，而当标签比例不变化时，效果最小 ($<$0.5\%)。我们希望这些发现和 RLSbench 的可用性将鼓励研究人员在宽松的标签转移设置中严格评估所提出的方法。代码可在 https://github.com/acmi-lab/RLSbench 上公开获取。 本文提出RLSbench基准测试平台，用于评估在放松标签偏移（Relaxed Label Shift）场景下的领域自适应方法，并设计了一种两步元算法以提升模型性能。 Saurabh Garg,Nick Erickson,James Sharpnack,Alex Smola,Sivaraman Balakrishnan,Zachary C. authors_text: Baxi Chong,Di Luo,Tianyu Wang,Gabriel Margolis,Juntao He,Pulkit Agrawal,Marin Soljačić,Daniel I. Goldman
title: Geometry of contact: contact planning for multi-legged robots via spin models duality
title_zh: 接触几何：通过旋转模型对偶性进行多足机器人的接触规划
comment: SI video: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.7608693
comments: SI video: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.7608693
abstract: Contact planning is crucial in locomoting systems.Specifically, appropriate contact planning can enable versatile behaviors (e.g., sidewinding in limbless locomotors) and facilitate speed-dependent gait transitions (e.g., walk-trot-gallop in quadrupedal locomotors). The challenges of contact planning include determining not only the sequence by which contact is made and broken between the locomotor and the environments, but also the sequence of internal shape changes (e.g., body bending and limb shoulder joint oscillation). Most state-of-art contact planning algorithms focused on conventional robots (e.g.biped and quadruped) and conventional tasks (e.g. forward locomotion), and there is a lack of study on general contact planning in multi-legged robots. In this paper, we show that using geometric mechanics framework, we can obtain the global optimal contact sequence given the internal shape changes sequence. Therefore, we simplify the contact planning problem to a graph optimization problem to identify the internal shape changes. Taking advantages of the spatio-temporal symmetry in locomotion, we map the graph optimization problem to special cases of spin models, which allows us to obtain the global optima in polynomial time. We apply our approach to develop new forward and sidewinding behaviors in a hexapod and a 12-legged centipede. We verify our predictions using numerical and robophysical models, and obtain novel and effective locomotion behaviors.
summary: Contact planning is crucial in locomoting systems.Specifically, appropriate contact planning can enable versatile behaviors (e.g., sidewinding in limbless locomotors) and facilitate speed-dependent gait transitions (e.g., walk-trot-gallop in quadrupedal locomotors). The challenges of contact planning include determining not only the sequence by which contact is made and broken between the locomotor and the environments, but also the sequence of internal shape changes (e.g., body bending and limb shoulder joint oscillation). Most state-of-art contact planning algorithms focused on conventional robots (e.g.biped and quadruped) and conventional tasks (e.g. forward locomotion), and there is a lack of study on general contact planning in multi-legged robots. In this paper, we show that using geometric mechanics framework, we can obtain the global optimal contact sequence given the internal shape changes sequence. Therefore, we simplify the contact planning problem to a graph optimization problem to identify the internal shape changes. Taking advantages of the spatio-temporal symmetry in locomotion, we map the graph optimization problem to special cases of spin models, which allows us to obtain the global optima in polynomial time. We apply our approach to develop new forward and sidewinding behaviors in a hexapod and a 12-legged centipede. We verify our predictions using numerical and robophysical models, and obtain novel and effective locomotion behaviors.
abstract_zh: 接触规划在运动系统中至关重要。具体来说，适当的接触规划可以实现多种行为（例如，无肢运动机中的侧绕）并促进速度相关的步态转换（例如，四足运动机中的步行-小跑-疾驰）。接触规划的挑战不仅包括确定运动器与环境之间建立和断开接触的顺序，还包括确定内部形状变化的顺序（例如，身体弯曲和肢体肩关节摆动）。大多数最先进的接触规划算法都集中在传统机器人（例如双足和四足）和传统任务（例如向前运动）上，并且缺乏对多足机器人中通用接触规划的研究。在本文中，我们表明使用几何力学框架，我们可以在给定内部形状变化序列的情况下获得全局最优接触序列。因此，我们将接触规划问题简化为图优化问题来识别内部形状变化。利用运动中的时空对称性，我们将图优化问题映射到自旋模型的特殊情况，这使我们能够在多项式时间内获得全局最优值。我们应用我们的方法来开发六足动物和十二足蜈蚣的新的向前和侧向行为。我们使用数值和机器人物理模型验证我们的预测，并获得新颖且有效的运动行为。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于几何力学框架的接触规划方法，通过将多足机器人接触序列优化转化为图优化问题，并利用自旋模型的时空对称性，实现全局最优解的高效求解。
AI.motivation: 现有接触规划算法多针对双足/四足机器人及简单任务，缺乏对多足机器人通用接触规划的研究，难以实现复杂运动模式（如侧向蠕动）和速度依赖的步态转换。
AI.method: 构建几何力学框架下的接触规划模型，将内部形态变化序列作为输入，通过图优化确定最优接触序列；利用运动的时空对称性，将问题映射为自旋模型特例，在多项式时间内求解全局最优解。
AI.result: 在六足机器人和12足蜈蚣机器人上实现了新型前进和侧向蠕动行为，通过数值模拟和机器人物理模型验证了方法的有效性，获得了新颖且高效的运动模式。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为多足机器人提供了通用接触规划框架，能够高效生成复杂运动行为，推动多足机器人在多样化地形中的应用。

authors_text: Tiange Xiang,Mahmut Yurt,Ali B Syed,Kawin Setsompop,Akshay Chaudhari
title: DDM$^2$: Self-Supervised Diffusion MRI Denoising with Generative Diffusion Models
title_zh: DDM$^2$：使用生成扩散模型进行自监督扩散 MRI 去噪
comment: To appear in ICLR 2023
comments: To appear in ICLR 2023
abstract: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is a common and life-saving medical imaging technique. However, acquiring high signal-to-noise ratio MRI scans requires long scan times, resulting in increased costs and patient discomfort, and decreased throughput. Thus, there is great interest in denoising MRI scans, especially for the subtype of diffusion MRI scans that are severely SNR-limited. While most prior MRI denoising methods are supervised in nature, acquiring supervised training datasets for the multitude of anatomies, MRI scanners, and scan parameters proves impractical. Here, we propose Denoising Diffusion Models for Denoising Diffusion MRI (DDM$^2$), a self-supervised denoising method for MRI denoising using diffusion denoising generative models. Our three-stage framework integrates statistic-based denoising theory into diffusion models and performs denoising through conditional generation. During inference, we represent input noisy measurements as a sample from an intermediate posterior distribution within the diffusion Markov chain. We conduct experiments on 4 real-world in-vivo diffusion MRI datasets and show that our DDM$^2$ demonstrates superior denoising performances ascertained with clinically-relevant visual qualitative and quantitative metrics.

abstract_zh: 磁共振成像（MRI）是一种常见且可挽救生命的医学成像技术。然而，获取高信噪比 MRI 扫描需要较长的扫描时间，从而导致成本增加、患者不适以及吞吐量降低。因此，人们对 MRI 扫描去噪非常感兴趣，特别是对于信噪比严重受限的扩散 MRI 扫描亚型。虽然大多数现有的 MRI 去噪方法本质上是有监督的，但获取大量解剖结构、MRI 扫描仪和扫描参数的有监督训练数据集被证明是不切实际的。在这里，我们提出了用于去噪扩散 MRI 的去噪扩散模型 (DDM$^2$)，这是一种使用扩散去噪生成模型进行 MRI 去噪的自监督去噪方法。我们的三阶段框架将基于统计的去噪理论集成到扩散模型中，并通过条件生成来执行去噪。在推理过程中，我们将输入噪声测量表示为来自扩散马尔可夫链内的中间后验分布的样本。我们对 4 个真实的体内扩散 MRI 数据集进行了实验，结果表明我们的 DDM$^2$ 通过临床相关的视觉定性和定量指标确定了卓越的去噪性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DDM²方法，利用生成扩散模型实现自监督的扩散MRI去噪。
AI.motivation: 高信噪比MRI扫描耗时长且成本高，传统监督去噪方法依赖难以获取的标注数据，需开发无需监督的去噪方案。
AI.method: 提出三阶段框架，融合统计去噪理论与扩散模型，通过条件生成实现去噪，并在推理阶段将噪声输入建模为扩散链的中间后验分布样本。
AI.result: 在4个真实扩散MRI数据集上，DDM²在临床相关指标下表现出优于现有方法的去噪性能。
AI.conclusion: DDM²验证了自监督扩散模型在医学影像去噪中的有效性，为多解剖结构和扫描参数场景提供通用解决方案。

However, acquiring high signal-to-noise ratio MRI scans requires long scan times, resulting in increased costs and patient discomfort, and decreased throughput. Thus, there is great interest in denoising MRI scans, especially for the subtype of diffusion MRI scans that are severely SNR-limited. While most prior MRI denoising methods are supervised in nature, acquiring supervised training datasets for the multitude of anatomies, MRI scanners, and scan parameters proves impractical. authors_text: Neel Kanwal,Roger Amundsen,Helga Hardardottir,Luca Tomasetti,Erling Sandoy Undersrud,Emiel A. M. Janssen,Kjersti Engan
title: Detection and Localization of Melanoma Skin Cancer in Histopathological Whole Slide Images
title_zh: 组织病理学全切片图像中黑色素瘤皮肤癌的检测和定位
comment: Accepted at EUSIPCO 23
comments: Accepted at EUSIPCO 23
abstract: Melanoma diagnosed and treated in its early stages can increase the survival rate. A projected increase in skin cancer incidents and a dearth of dermatopathologists have emphasized the need for computational pathology (CPATH) systems. CPATH systems with deep learning (DL) models have the potential to identify the presence of melanoma by exploiting underlying morphological and cellular features. This paper proposes a DL method to detect melanoma and distinguish between normal skin and benign/malignant melanocytic lesions in Whole Slide Images (WSI). Our method detects lesions with high accuracy and localizes them on a WSI to identify potential regions of interest for pathologists. Interestingly, our DL method relies on using a single CNN network to create localization maps first and use them to perform slide-level predictions to determine patients who have melanoma. Our best model provides favorable patch-wise classification results with a 0.992 F1 score and 0.99 sensitivity on unseen data. The source code is https://github.com/RogerAmundsen/Melanoma-Diagnosis-and-Localization-from-Whole-Slide-Images-using-Convolutional-Neural-Networks.
summary: Melanoma diagnosed and treated in its early stages can increase the survival rate. A projected increase in skin cancer incidents and a dearth of dermatopathologists have emphasized the need for computational pathology (CPATH) systems. CPATH systems with deep learning (DL) models have the potential to identify the presence of melanoma by exploiting underlying morphological and cellular features. This paper proposes a DL method to detect melanoma and distinguish between normal skin and benign/malignant melanocytic lesions in Whole Slide Images (WSI). Our method detects lesions with high accuracy and localizes them on a WSI to identify potential regions of interest for pathologists. Interestingly, our DL method relies on using a single CNN network to create localization maps first and use them to perform slide-level predictions to determine patients who have melanoma. Our best model provides favorable patch-wise classification results with a 0.992 F1 score and 0.99 sensitivity on unseen data. The source code is https://github.com/RogerAmundsen/Melanoma-Diagnosis-and-Localization-from-Whole-Slide-Images-using-Convolutional-Neural-Networks.
abstract_zh: 黑色素瘤的早期诊断和治疗可以提高生存率。皮肤癌事件的预计增加和皮肤病理学家的缺乏强调了计算病理学 (CPATH) 系统的必要性。具有深度学习 (DL) 模型的 CPATH 系统有可能通过利用潜在的形态和细胞特征来识别黑色素瘤的存在。本文提出了一种在全幻灯片图像（WSI）中检测黑色素瘤并区分正常皮肤和良性/恶性黑色素细胞病变的深度学习方法。我们的方法可以高精度检测病变，并将其定位在 WSI 上，以确定病理学家潜在的感兴趣区域。有趣的是，我们的深度学习方法首先依赖于使用单个 CNN 网络创建定位图，然后使用它们执行幻灯片级预测来确定患有黑色素瘤的患者。我们的最佳模型提供了良好的 patch-wise 分类结果，F1 得分为 0.992，对未见数据的敏感性为 0.99。源代码是 https://github.com/RogerAmundsen/Melanoma-Diagnosis-and-Localization-from-Whole-Slide-Images-using-Convolutional-Neural-Networks。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度学习的方法，在组织病理学全切片图像（WSI）中检测和定位黑色素瘤皮肤癌，并区分正常皮肤与良性/恶性黑色素细胞病变。
AI.motivation: 早期诊断黑色素瘤可提高患者生存率，但皮肤癌病例增加及皮肤病理学家短缺促使开发计算病理学系统。现有方法在病变检测和定位上存在不足，需更高效的解决方案。
AI.method: 采用单一卷积神经网络（CNN）生成定位图，通过滑动级预测区分患者是否患有黑色素瘤，结合病变区域定位与分类任务。
AI.result: 最佳模型在未见数据上实现0.992的F1分数和0.99的敏感度，代码已开源（https://github.com/RogerAmundsen/Melanoma-Diagnosis-and-Localization-from-Whole-Slide-Images-using-Convolutional-Neural-Networks）。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在黑色素瘤检测与定位任务中表现优异，为病理学家提供高效辅助工具，未来可进一步优化模型泛化能力与临床应用适配性。

authors_text: Dominic Widdows,Jyoti Rani,Emmanuel Pothos
title: Quantum Circuit Components for Cognitive Decision-Making
title_zh: 用于认知决策的量子电路组件
abstract: This paper demonstrates that some non-classical models of human decision-making can be run successfully as circuits on quantum computers. Since the 1960s, many observed cognitive behaviors have been shown to violate rules based on classical probability and set theory. For example, the order in which questions are posed in a survey affects whether participants answer 'yes' or 'no', so the population that answers 'yes' to both questions cannot be modeled as the intersection of two fixed sets. It can, however, be modeled as a sequence of projections carried out in different orders. This and other examples have been described successfully using quantum probability, which relies on comparing angles between subspaces rather than volumes between subsets. Now in the early 2020s, quantum computers have reached the point where some of these quantum cognitive models can be implemented and investigated on quantum hardware, by representing the mental states in qubit registers, and the cognitive operations and decisions using different gates and measurements. This paper develops such quantum circuit representations for quantum cognitive models, focusing particularly on modeling order effects and decision-making under uncertainty. The claim is not that the human brain uses qubits and quantum circuits explicitly (just like the use of Boolean set theory does not require the brain to be using classical bits), but that the mathematics shared between quantum cognition and quantum computing motivates the exploration of quantum computers for cognition modeling. Key quantum properties include superposition, entanglement, and collapse, as these mathematical elements provide a common language between cognitive models, quantum hardware, and circuit implementations.

The claim is not that the human brain uses qubits and quantum circuits explicitly (just like the use of Boolean set theory does not require the brain to be using classical bits), but that the mathematics shared between quantum cognition and quantum computing motivates the exploration of quantum computers for cognition modeling. Key quantum properties include superposition, entanglement, and collapse, as these mathematical elements provide a common language between cognitive models, quantum hardware, and circuit implementations.
abstract_zh: 本文证明了人类决策的一些非经典模型可以作为电路在量子计算机上成功运行。自 20 世纪 60 年代以来，许多观察到的认知行为已被证明违反了基于经典概率和集合论的规则。例如，调查中提出问题的顺序会影响参与者回答“是”或“否”，因此对两个问题都回答“是”的人口不能建模为两个固定集的交集。然而，它可以被建模为以不同顺序执行的一系列投影。这个例子和其他例子已经使用量子概率成功地描述了，量子概率依赖于比较子空间之间的角度而不是子集之间的体积。现在，在 2020 年代初，量子计算机已经达到了这样的程度：通过在量子位寄存器中表示心理状态，以及使用不同的门和测量进行认知操作和决策，可以在量子硬件上实现和研究其中一些量子认知模型。本文为量子认知模型开发了这种量子电路表示，特别关注不确定性下的有序效应和决策建模。这种说法并不是说人脑明确地使用量子位和量子电路（就像使用布尔集合论并不要求大脑使用经典位一样），而是说量子认知和量子计算之间共享的数学激发了量子计算机对认知建模的探索。关键的量子特性包括叠加、纠缠和塌缩，因为这些数学元素提供了认知模型、量子硬件和电路实现之间的通用语言。

AI.tldr: 本文展示了非经典人类决策模型可通过量子电路在量子计算机上实现，重点研究顺序效应和不确定性决策的建模。
AI.motivation: 经典概率模型无法解释认知行为中的顺序效应等现象，而量子概率理论能通过子空间角度比较提供更优描述，量子计算机的发展使该理论可被硬件验证。
AI.method: 将心理状态映射到量子位寄存器，使用量子门与测量操作模拟认知过程，构建量子电路实现量子认知模型。
AI.result: 成功开发了量子电路表示方法，在量子硬件上验证了顺序效应和不确定性决策的量子认知模型。
AI.conclusion: 量子计算与认知模型共享数学语言，尽管人脑未必使用量子机制，但量子计算机可作为认知建模的新工具。

search_text: Quantum Circuit Components for Cognitive Decision-Making 用于认知决策的量子电路组件 This paper demonstrates that some non-classical models of human decision-making can be run successfully as circuits on quantum computers. Since the 1960s, many observed cognitive behaviors have been shown to violate rules based on classical probability and set theory. For example, the order in which questions are posed in a survey affects whether participants answer 'yes' or 'no', so the population that answers 'yes' to both questions cannot be modeled as the intersection of two fixed sets. It can, however, be modeled as a sequence of projections carried out in different orders. This and other examples have been described successfully using quantum probability, which relies on comparing angles between subspaces rather than volumes between subsets. Now in the early 2020s, quantum computers have reached the point where some of these quantum cognitive models can be implemented and investigated on quantum hardware, by representing the mental states in qubit registers, and the cognitive operations and decisions using different gates and measurements. This paper develops such quantum circuit representations for quantum cognitive models, focusing particularly on modeling order effects and decision-making under uncertainty. The claim is not that the human brain uses qubits and quantum circuits explicitly (just like the use of Boolean set theory does not require the brain to be using classical bits), but that the mathematics shared between quantum cognition and quantum computing motivates the exploration of quantum computers for cognition modeling. Key quantum properties include superposition, entanglement, and collapse, as these mathematical elements provide a common language between cognitive models, quantum hardware, and circuit implementations. 本文证明了人类决策的一些非经典模型可以作为电路在量子计算机上成功运行。自 20 世纪 60 年代以来，许多观察到的认知行为已被证明违反了基于经典概率和集合论的规则。例如，调查中提出问题的顺序会影响参与者回答“是”或“否”，因此对两个问题都回答“是”的人口不能建模为两个固定集的交集。然而，它可以被建模为以不同顺序执行的一系列投影。这个例子和其他例子已经使用量子概率成功地描述了，量子概率依赖于比较子空间之间的角度而不是子集之间的体积。现在，在 2020 年代初，量子计算机已经达到了这样的程度：通过在量子位寄存器中表示心理状态，以及使用不同的门和测量进行认知操作和决策，可以在量子硬件上实现和研究其中一些量子认知模型。本文为量子认知模型开发了这种量子电路表示，特别关注不确定性下的有序效应和决策建模。这种说法并不是说人脑明确地使用量子位和量子电路（就像使用布尔集合论并不要求大脑使用经典位一样），而是说量子认知和量子计算之间共享的数学激发了量子计算机对认知建模的探索。关键的量子特性包括叠加、纠缠和塌缩，因为这些数学元素提供了认知模型、量子硬件和电路实现之间的通用语言。 本文展示了非经典人类决策模型可通过量子电路在量子计算机上实现，重点研究顺序效应和不确定性决策的建模。 Dominic Widdows,Jyoti Rani,Emmanuel Pothos quant-ph,cs.AI

authors_text: Patrick Esser,Johnathan Chiu,Parmida Atighehchian,Jonathan Granskog,Anastasis Germanidis
title: Structure and Content-Guided Video Synthesis with Diffusion Models
title_zh: 使用扩散模型的结构和内容引导视频合成
comment: Project page at https://research.runwayml.com/gen1
comments: Project page at https://research.runwayml.com/gen1
abstract: Text-guided generative diffusion models unlock powerful image creation and editing tools. While these have been extended to video generation, current approaches that edit the content of existing footage while retaining structure require expensive re-training for every input or rely on error-prone propagation of image edits across frames. In this work, we present a structure and content-guided video diffusion model that edits videos based on visual or textual descriptions of the desired output. Conflicts between user-provided content edits and structure representations occur due to insufficient disentanglement between the two aspects. As a solution, we show that training on monocular depth estimates with varying levels of detail provides control over structure and content fidelity. Our model is trained jointly on images and videos which also exposes explicit control of temporal consistency through a novel guidance method. Our experiments demonstrate a wide variety of successes; fine-grained control over output characteristics, customization based on a few reference images, and a strong user preference towards results by our model.
summary: Text-guided generative diffusion models unlock powerful image creation and editing tools. While these have been extended to video generation, current approaches that edit the content of existing footage while retaining structure require expensive re-training for every input or rely on error-prone propagation of image edits across frames. In this work, we present a structure and content-guided video diffusion model that edits videos based on visual or textual descriptions of the desired output. Conflicts between user-provided content edits and structure representations occur due to insufficient disentanglement between the two aspects. As a solution, we show that training on monocular depth estimates with varying levels of detail provides control over structure and content fidelity. Our model is trained jointly on images and videos which also exposes explicit control of temporal consistency through a novel guidance method. Our experiments demonstrate a wide variety of successes; fine-grained control over output characteristics, customization based on a few reference images, and a strong user preference towards results by our model.
abstract_zh: 文本引导的生成扩散模型解锁了强大的图像创建和编辑工具。虽然这些已扩展到视频生成，但当前在保留结构的同时编辑现有素材内容的方法需要对每个输入进行昂贵的重新训练，或者依赖于容易出错的跨帧图像编辑传播。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种结构和内容引导的视频传播模型，该模型根据所需输出的视觉或文本描述来编辑视频。用户提供的内容编辑和结构表示之间的冲突是由于两个方面之间的分离不充分而发生的。作为一种解决方案，我们表明，对具有不同细节级别的单目深度估计进行训练可以提供对结构和内容保真度的控制。我们的模型是在图像和视频上联合训练的，这还通过一种新颖的指导方法公开了对时间一致性的显式控制。我们的实验取得了广泛的成功；对输出特性的细粒度控制、基于一些参考图像的定制以及用户对我们模型的结果的强烈偏好。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于结构和内容引导的视频扩散模型，能够根据视觉或文本描述对视频进行编辑，同时保持结构和内容的保真度。
AI.motivation: 现有视频生成方法在编辑现有视频内容时，需要昂贵的重新训练或依赖容易出错的帧间编辑传播，无法有效分离结构与内容信息。
AI.method: 通过训练单目深度估计（不同细节水平）控制结构与内容保真度，并联合图像和视频训练，结合新型引导方法实现时间一致性控制。
AI.result: 实现了精细的输出控制、基于少量参考图像的定制化生成，且用户偏好度显著高于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 该模型通过解耦结构与内容信息，为视频编辑提供了灵活且高效的解决方案，具有较高的用户满意度。

authors_text: Nooshin Yousefzadeh,Charlie Tran,Adolfo Ramirez-Zamora,Jinghua Chen,Ruogu Fang,My T. Thai
title: LAVA: Granular Neuron-Level Explainable AI for Alzheimer's Disease Assessment from Fundus Images
title_zh: LAVA：通过眼底图像评估阿尔茨海默病的颗粒神经元级可解释人工智能
comment: 27 pages, 11 figures
comments: 27 pages, 11 figures
abstract: Alzheimer's Disease (AD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disease and the leading cause of dementia. Early diagnosis is critical for patients to benefit from potential intervention and treatment. The retina has been hypothesized as a diagnostic site for AD detection owing to its anatomical connection with the brain. Developed AI models for this purpose have yet to provide a rational explanation about the decision and neither infer the stage of disease's progression. Along this direction, we propose a novel model-agnostic explainable-AI framework, called Granular Neuron-level Explainer (LAVA), an interpretation prototype that probes into intermediate layers of the Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) models to assess the AD continuum directly from the retinal imaging without longitudinal or clinical evaluation. This method is applied to validate the retinal vasculature as a biomarker and diagnostic modality for Alzheimer's Disease (AD) evaluation. UK Biobank cognitive tests and vascular morphological features suggest LAVA shows strong promise and effectiveness in identifying AD stages across the progression continuum.
summary: Alzheimer's Disease (AD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disease and the leading cause of dementia. Early diagnosis is critical for patients to benefit from potential intervention and treatment. The retina has been hypothesized as a diagnostic site for AD detection owing to its anatomical connection with the brain. Developed AI models for this purpose have yet to provide a rational explanation about the decision and neither infer the stage of disease's progression. Along this direction, we propose a novel model-agnostic explainable-AI framework, called Granular Neuron-level Explainer (LAVA), an interpretation prototype that probes into intermediate layers of the Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) models to assess the AD continuum directly from the retinal imaging without longitudinal or clinical evaluation. This method is applied to validate the retinal vasculature as a biomarker and diagnostic modality for Alzheimer's Disease (AD) evaluation. UK Biobank cognitive tests and vascular morphological features suggest LAVA shows strong promise and effectiveness in identifying AD stages across the progression continuum.
abstract_zh: 阿尔茨海默病（AD）是一种进行性神经退行性疾病，也是痴呆症的主要原因。早期诊断对于患者从潜在的干预和治疗中受益至关重要。由于视网膜与大脑的解剖学联系，因此被假设为 AD 检测的诊断部位。为此开发的人工智能模型尚未对这一决定提供合理的解释，也无法推断疾病进展的阶段。沿着这个方向，我们提出了一种新的与模型无关的可解释人工智能框架，称为颗粒神经元级解释器（LAVA），这是一种解释原型，可探究卷积神经网络（CNN）模型的中间层，直接从视网膜成像评估 AD 连续体，无需纵向或临床评估。该方法用于验证视网膜脉管系统作为阿尔茨海默病 (AD) 评估的生物标志物和诊断方式。英国生物银行认知测试和血管形态特征表明，LAVA 在识别整个进展过程中的 AD 阶段方面显示出强大的前景和有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为LAVA的可解释AI框架，用于通过视网膜图像直接评估阿尔茨海默病（AD）的进展阶段。
AI.motivation: 阿尔茨海默病早期诊断对干预治疗至关重要，但现有AI模型缺乏决策解释且无法推断疾病阶段。视网膜与大脑的解剖连接使其成为潜在诊断部位，但需开发可解释方法验证其有效性。
AI.method: LAVA是一种模型无关的解释框架，通过分析卷积神经网络（CNN）中间层的神经元激活，直接从视网膜图像中评估AD进展，无需纵向或临床数据。
AI.result: 基于UK Biobank的认知测试和血管形态学特征验证，LAVA在AD进展连续体中有效识别疾病阶段，表明视网膜血管可作为AD的生物标志物。
AI.conclusion: LAVA为AD评估提供了粒度神经元级别的可解释AI方法，验证了视网膜血管作为诊断标志物的潜力，具有临床应用前景。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为LAVA的可解释AI框架，用于通过视网膜图像直接评估阿尔茨海默病（AD）的进展阶段。
ai_content.motivation: 阿尔茨海默病早期诊断对干预治疗至关重要，但现有AI模型缺乏决策解释且无法推断疾病阶段。视网膜与大脑的解剖连接使其成为潜在诊断部位，但需开发可解释方法验证其有效性。
ai_content.method: LAVA是一种模型无关的解释框架，通过分析卷积神经网络（CNN）中间层的神经元激活，直接从视网膜图像中评估AD进展，无需纵向或临床数据。
ai_content.result: 基于UK Biobank的认知测试和血管形态学特征验证，LAVA在AD进展连续体中有效识别疾病阶段，表明视网膜血管可作为AD的生物标志物。
ai_content.conclusion: LAVA为AD评估提供了粒度神经元级别的可解释AI方法，验证了视网膜血管作为诊断标志物的潜力，具有临床应用前景。

search_text: LAVA: Granular Neuron-Level Explainable AI for Alzheimer's Disease Assessment from Fundus Images LAVA：通过眼底图像评估阿尔茨海默病的颗粒神经元级可解释人工智能 Alzheimer's Disease (AD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disease and the leading cause of dementia. Early diagnosis is critical for patients to benefit from potential intervention and treatment. The retina has been hypothesized as a diagnostic site for AD detection owing to its anatomical connection with the brain. Developed AI models for this purpose have yet to provide a rational explanation about the decision and neither infer the stage of disease's progression. Along this direction, we propose a novel model-agnostic explainable-AI framework, called Granular Neuron-level Explainer (LAVA), an interpretation prototype that probes into intermediate layers of the Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) models to assess the AD continuum directly from the retinal imaging without longitudinal or clinical evaluation. This method is applied to validate the retinal vasculature as a biomarker and diagnostic modality for Alzheimer's Disease (AD) evaluation. UK Biobank cognitive tests and vascular morphological features suggest LAVA shows strong promise and effectiveness in identifying AD stages across the progression continuum. 阿尔茨海默病（AD）是一种进行性神经退行性疾病，也是痴呆症的主要原因。早期诊断对于患者从潜在的干预和治疗中受益至关重要。由于视网膜与大脑的解剖学联系，因此被假设为 AD 检测的诊断部位。为此开发的人工智能模型尚未对这一决定提供合理的解释，也无法推断疾病进展的阶段。沿着这个方向，我们提出了一种新的与模型无关的可解释人工智能框架，称为颗粒神经元级解释器（LAVA），这是一种解释原型，可探究卷积神经网络（CNN）模型的中间层，直接从视网膜成像评估 AD 连续体，无需纵向或临床评估。该方法用于验证视网膜脉管系统作为阿尔茨海默病 (AD) 评估的生物标志物和诊断方式。英国生物银行认知测试和血管形态特征表明，LAVA 在识别整个进展过程中的 AD 阶段方面显示出强大的前景和有效性。 本文提出了一种名为LAVA的可解释AI框架，用于通过视网膜图像直接评估阿尔茨海默病（AD）的进展阶段。 Nooshin Yousefzadeh,Charlie Tran,Adolfo Ramirez-Zamora,Jinghua Chen,Ruogu Fang,My T. Thai cs.LG,cs.AI,eess.IV

authors_text: Yibo Yang,Haobo Yuan,Xiangtai Li,Zhouchen Lin,Philip Torr,Dacheng Tao
title: Neural Collapse Inspired Feature-Classifier Alignment for Few-Shot Class Incremental Learning
title_zh: 神经崩溃启发特征分类器对齐，实现少样本类增量学习
comment: ICLR 2023 (Notable-top-25%)
comments: ICLR 2023 (Notable-top-25%)
abstract: Few-shot class-incremental learning (FSCIL) has been a challenging problem as only a few training samples are accessible for each novel class in the new sessions. Finetuning the backbone or adjusting the classifier prototypes trained in the prior sessions would inevitably cause a misalignment between the feature and classifier of old classes, which explains the well-known catastrophic forgetting problem. In this paper, we deal with this misalignment dilemma in FSCIL inspired by the recently discovered phenomenon named neural collapse, which reveals that the last-layer features of the same class will collapse into a vertex, and the vertices of all classes are aligned with the classifier prototypes, which are formed as a simplex equiangular tight frame (ETF). It corresponds to an optimal geometric structure for classification due to the maximized Fisher Discriminant Ratio. We propose a neural collapse inspired framework for FSCIL. A group of classifier prototypes are pre-assigned as a simplex ETF for the whole label space, including the base session and all the incremental sessions. During training, the classifier prototypes are not learnable, and we adopt a novel loss function that drives the features into their corresponding prototypes. Theoretical analysis shows that our method holds the neural collapse optimality and does not break the feature-classifier alignment in an incremental fashion. Experiments on the miniImageNet, CUB-200, and CIFAR-100 datasets demonstrate that our proposed framework outperforms the state-of-the-art performances. Code address: https://github.com/NeuralCollapseApplications/FSCIL
